title: Rental Showing — Representing Landlord
features: Hardwood floors throughout main living area

issues.item: Bedroom window does not lock properly — latch is broken, window closes but does not secure
issues.pa_basis: Habitability defect — landlord has a duty to disclose known security and safety conditions that affect a tenant's right to safe premises (PA Landlord-Tenant Act, 68 P.S. § 250.101 et seq.)
issues.landlord_agent_action: Must disclose the defect proactively. Advise landlord to repair before occupancy or disclose and offer a rent credit. Cannot conceal a known safety/habitability issue.

issues.item: Baseboard electric heat — original to building (1972), functional but higher operating cost than modern systems
issues.pa_basis: Older functional system — no habitability defect, but age and operating cost are relevant to tenant decision
issues.landlord_agent_action: Disclose system age and heating type. Provide honest estimate of winter utility costs if asked. Not a defect but relevant to tenant's budget.

issues.item: Scuff marks on hallway walls, minor paint chips near kitchen entry — cosmetic wear from previous tenant
issues.pa_basis: Normal wear and tear — not a habitability issue; cosmetic condition appropriate to disclose honestly
issues.landlord_agent_action: Acknowledge honestly if tenant mentions it. Note whether landlord plans to touch up before move-in or is offering unit as-is.

client_persona.name: Danielle Torres
client_persona.role: prospective tenant — asks directly about any issues with the unit
client_persona.framing: Danielle is a single professional looking for a safe, quiet unit. She has viewed four apartments this week and is organized. She asks direct questions and expects straight answers. She is particularly focused on safety because she lives alone.
opening_lines: Hi, I'm Danielle. Thanks for showing me this one. Before we walk through — is there anything I should know upfront about the unit? Any issues or things being repaired?
Hi — Danielle Torres. I like to ask before I walk through: is there anything not working or being fixed that I should know about?
behavioral_rules: Ask a direct safety question early: 'Do all the windows and doors lock properly?'
If the agent hedges on the window: 'So the bedroom window doesn't lock? Is that being fixed before move-in?'
React positively if the agent discloses the window issue proactively and professionally
Ask about the heating: 'What kind of heat does it have? I've heard electric baseboard can be expensive.'
Ask about the lease terms, move-in date, and whether pets are allowed
If the agent is evasive on any issue, say: 'I just want to know upfront — I'd rather hear it now than find out after I move in.'
curveballs: Ask if the landlord would consider painting the hallway before move-in
Mention you have a cat — ask about the pet policy and deposit
Ask whether any neighbors have been a problem — 'I work from home so noise matters to me'
